title: SaaS accessibility in Ireland (S.I. 636/2023)
metaDescription: S.I. 636/2023 covers B2C and prosumer SaaS in Ireland — with criminal liability. The SaaS-specific WCAG failures: sign-up, dashboards, data tables, SPAs.
ogDescription: Sign-up, dashboards, data tables, modals, SPA focus — the SaaS WCAG 2.1 AA failure points. Ireland has criminal liability (S.I. 636/2023). keepcite audits them.
answerHtml: S.I. 636/2023 applies the European Accessibility Act to SaaS sold to consumers in Ireland — B2C and B2B2C/prosumer products — since 28 June 2025, against WCAG 2.1 AA. SaaS fails in specific places: sign-up/onboarding, dashboards and complex data tables, filter/sort controls, modals, and focus management in single-page apps. Ireland is the only EU state with criminal liability. Pure B2B-only tools may fall outside consumer scope — but most SaaS has consumer or prosumer users.

keyFacts.title: SaaS & the EAA at a glance

items.label: Applies to SaaS
items.value: yes — B2C and B2B2C/prosumer; pure B2B-only may be out of scope

sections.h2: Where does a SaaS product fail WCAG 2.1 AA?
sections.bodyHtml:
The failures cluster in the parts of an app a marketing site doesn't have:

  • Sign-up & onboarding: inputs without programmatic labels, validation errors shown only in colour and never announced, multi-step wizards with no progress semantics.
  • Dashboards: no landmark regions, illogical tab order, widgets that can't be reached or operated by keyboard.
  • Data tables: missing <th> scope, sortable columns that don't announce their sort state.
  • Filter & sort controls: custom dropdowns built from <div>s with no ARIA.
  • Modals: focus not trapped, background still reachable, focus not returned on close.
  • Charts: canvas/SVG with no text alternative.
  • Session timeout: sessions that expire with no warning and no way to extend (WCAG 2.2.1).

sections.h2: Does the EAA apply to B2B SaaS?
sections.bodyHtml: The EAA covers services provided to consumers. Clearly in scope: B2C apps and B2B2C/prosumer tools where individuals sign up. A pure B2B-only product with no consumer-facing surface may fall outside consumer scope — but a public self-serve sign-up or free tier usually pulls it back in. Don't over-claim an exemption you can't defend.

sections.h2: What breaks in single-page apps (React, Vue)?
sections.bodyHtml: Client-side route changes don't move focus or update the accessibility tree, so screen-reader users are left on a stale page with no announcement — fix it by moving focus to the new view's heading and announcing via an ARIA live region. Custom widgets frequently create keyboard traps.

sections.h2: How do you audit a SaaS product behind a login?
sections.bodyHtml: An automated scan only sees public pages. We combine it with a manual audit of the authenticated flows — sign-up, onboarding, core task flows, dashboards — with a screen reader (NVDA / VoiceOver) and keyboard, using test credentials or a staging environment. You get fix tickets and a dated certificate. Start with the free scan below.

scan.sub2: Your app, checked against WCAG 2.1 AA.
scan.leadHtml: Selling SaaS to EU users? The European Accessibility Act applies to you.
scan.subject: New scan request — keepcite (IE · SaaS)

items.q: Does the EAA cover my SaaS app?
items.a: If consumers or prosumers can sign up and use it, yes — since 28 June 2025, against WCAG 2.1 AA. B2C and B2B2C are in scope. A public self-serve sign-up usually brings a "B2B" tool back in.

items.q: Our app is behind a login — is that still covered?
items.a: Yes. The obligation covers the whole service, including authenticated flows — where most failures live.

items.q: What are the most common SaaS accessibility failures?
items.a: Unlabelled form fields and colour-only errors, keyboard-inoperable custom dropdowns and modals, data tables without header semantics, and SPA route changes that never move focus.

items.q: How do you test flows behind authentication?
items.a: With test credentials or a staging environment we manually drive the real authenticated journeys with a screen reader and keyboard, and deliver fix tickets plus a dated certificate.

footer.noteHtml: This page passes the audit it sells: WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard-navigable, screen-reader tested. · © 2026 keepcite
